text.title: Supply Chain Security Core Rules
text.summary: 5+ testable rules grounding the methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Includes the skip-this-methodology rule that the decision-tree maps to.

rule.statement: Secret scanning MUST run on every commit via pre-commit hook (Gitleaks or detect-secrets) AND in CI on the full history (`git fetch --unshallow`). When a secret lands on main, rewrite history with `git filter-repo` and rotate the credential immediately.

rationale.source: v1-source: Gitleaks docs; faion-network security audit Q1 2026

rule.rationale: Pre-commit catches secrets before they reach the remote. CI with full history catches secrets introduced by force-pushes, merge commits, or squash merges that bypass the hook. Rotation without history rewrite leaves the secret in the public git object store indefinitely.

rule.statement: Use lock files (package-lock.json, poetry.lock, go.sum, Cargo.lock) for all dependency manifests. Commit lock files. Never add lock files to .gitignore.
rule.rationale: Lock files pin the exact resolved dependency tree including transitive dependencies, preventing silent upgrades to vulnerable versions during CI installs. Without lock files, `npm install` today and tomorrow may resolve different packages.

rule.statement: License policy: deny by default; maintain an allowlist of approved licenses (MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-3-Clause, MPL-2.0). Reject GPL-3.0 and AGPL-3.0 for commercial projects unless legal sign-off is explicit.
rule.rationale: SCA tools struggle with vendored or unknown-license code; an explicit allowlist forces review rather than silent acceptance of copyleft licenses that can create legal obligations.

rule.statement: Generate an SBOM (Syft or Trivy, SPDX or CycloneDX format) per build and store it as a release artefact. SBOM component-count regression vs prior build fails CI.

rationale.source: v1-source: CISA Software Bill of Materials guidance

rule.rationale: Without per-build SBOMs there is no audit trail for what shipped. Regression detection catches accidental dependency loss as well as malicious tree changes.

rule.statement: Apply Supply Chain Security to one repository per pass. Cross-repo passes MUST be split. Methodology MUST be skipped when there is no incident response path — adding more findings without a triage workflow makes the team blind, not safer.
rule.rationale: Anti-conflation: batched passes lose per-repo context. Findings without triage create noise that erodes trust in the scanner.
